metadata.description: PDF catalog modules matched to Lectures.xlsx with lectures, YouTube links, and transcripts

modules.name: Basics of Continuous Auditing and Continuous Monitoring
modules.description: Continuous auditing is the product of electronic data processing and audit automation. This module is designed to give an introductory overview of the continuous auditing framework. The module illustrates how the continuous audit framework is evolving over time, the stages and processes of continuous audit, and how continuous audit is different from the traditional audit.
modules.matched_lecture_name: Basics of Continuous Auditing and Continuous Monitoring

modules.name: Basics of Artificial Intelligence in Business
modules.description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) and its underlying component, Machine Learning (ML), are integrated into every facet of our lives – from healthcare to our homes. Many companies have relied on AI to drive the success of their businesses. This module introduces students to the benefits of AI and shows its current business applications and potential uses in the business domains. Each of the major AI types is covered in detail with descriptions and examples.
modules.matched_lecture_name: Basics of Artificial Intelligence in Business

modules.name: Introduction to Process Mining
modules.description: Process mining is a technique that extracts information from event logs to discover, monitor and improve business processes. This module is designed to give an introductory overview of process mining and explain its application using software. The module illustrates how to define process mining and use it to interpret and process data, and further describes the techniques that help extract information from event logs to discover, monitor, and improve business processes. This module also demonstrates how to utilize process mining technique by providing a real data example of the RADAR (Rutgers AICPA Data Analytics Research) project at CAR (Continuous Auditing and Reporting) Lab.

modules.name: Use of Classifiers in Audit
modules.description: This module introduces various classification models, including, decision trees and K-Nearest Neighbors (K-NN), and explains how to build them. It further covers ensemble methods of classifiers and their benefits when utilized in the model. The evaluation of the classifiers is performed through a variety of methods.

modules.name: Analyses of Exceptions and Anomalies
modules.description: Statistical methods for exception detection in traditional auditing do not fit well into continuous auditing. Generally, the number of identified exceptions is too large to investigate in its entirety. The module explains how the concept of Exceptional Exceptions works to mitigate information overload encountered in the conventional method. Following exception identification, this methodology provides a risk-based exception prioritization method to rank those exceptions in the order of suspicion. The material covers the internal logic of the methodology, analytics steps, and relevant algorithms. Use cases are presented to better understand the techniques.

modules.name: Duplicate Detection Techniques
modules.description: Duplicate records in databases may lead to problems, but their causes vary. Duplicate detection plays a vital role in data analytics. This module covers the steps of two methods to detect duplicates. It focuses on the duplicate payments problem in accounting and illustrates the application of detection methods to duplicate payments. The accuracy and evaluation of the technique are also covered.

modules.name: Introduction to Audit Automation
modules.description: Business Process Automation was designed to carry out repetitive tasks that allow humans to solve complex business problems. The use of Robotic Process Automation (RPA) in audit procedures can give rise to a more efficient and accurate auditing process. This hands-on module demonstrates the effectiveness of RPA and its application in accounting, auditing, and data analytics.

modules.name: Emerging Audit Evidence
modules.description: This module takes a holistic view of accounting and finance and the evolution from traditional to digital models. These changes necessitate new and more relevant ways of approaching the profession and dealing with the challenges associated with technology-driven finance and accounting business models. This module provides a brief overview of different technology types, and then details their use and business implications.

modules.name: Data Preprocessing and Manipulations for Audit
modules.description: This module covers basic Excel exercises that teach students to manipulate data for a variety of purposes. Students learn how to properly format the data for further analysis and how to use simple formulas in Excel. The module covers the simplest but most common features of spreadsheets used in the business world.
modules.matched_lecture_name: Data Preprocessing and Manipulations for Audit (Excel Workshop)

modules.name: Introduction to Data Visualization and Visual Analytics
modules.description: Visualization tools and techniques provide enhanced understanding of data and processes, and as such can provide powerful evidence for communicating an accounting and finance story. Visual analytics provide insights and helps identify patterns in the data. This hands- on module introduces the statistical package R, its common variable types and charts, and demonstrates the R codes for chart visualization, and visualization functions of Tableau. It further explains the differences between exploratory and explanatory visualizations.

modules.name: Interactive and Dynamic Graphs and Dashboards
modules.description: This module covers additional data visualization concepts via a hands-on visualization case and an accompanying recorded videos to introduce students to conducting ratio analysis using visualization software. Students learn to create different types of graphs and dynamic dashboards that include multiple interactive graphs.
modules.matched_lecture_name: Interactive and Dynamic Graphs and Dashboards

modules.name: Sentiment Analysis and Regular Expression in Text Mining
modules.description: This module discusses how auditors can use text mining to retrieve unstructured data on accounting-related materials, and how the information can provide valuable insights. In addition, it helps them understand ways to compare various contextual factors, including lexical bundle analysis, and learn to extract and analyze potential data such as financial statements, news articles, internal emails, and customer voice. It further highlights the importance of internet data (Twitter, LinkedIn, Facebook) in the understanding market and product trends for accounting and finance (Sentiment analysis).
modules.matched_lecture_name: Sentiment Analysis and Regular Expression in Text Mining

lectures.lecture_name: Intro to Audit Analytics: A Primer for Accounting in Text Analytic

modules.name: Introduction to Blockchain and Smart Contracts
modules.description: This module presents the background of blockchain technology and its internal design, including how hash functions and chain structure work in blockchain. The module also illustrates how a smart contract is designed with blockchain technology and its application in business, and particularly in accounting.
modules.matched_lecture_name: Introduction to Blockchain and Smart Contracts

modules.name: Introduction to Crypto Currencies
modules.description: This module explains the purpose and use of crypto currency, and its impact on the global economy. It addresses the key technologies used to build a crypto currency system, and the properties that differentiate crypto currencies from traditional currencies. It further reviews the crypto currency market situation and associated evolving accounting principles, including legislation and its implications for accounting and finance.

modules.name: Basics of Cybersecurity
modules.description: The widespread use of technology has created many efficiencies for accounting and finance while simultaneously creating challenges related to ethics and privacy. This module discusses the importance of cybersecurity and the challenges associated with a complex cyber environment. It further introduces good practice standards, organizations, and frameworks of cybersecurity management.

modules.name: RPA Fundamentals: UiPath I
modules.description: This module uses UiPath, a leading automation software, to demonstrate the Robotic Process Automation technology. In this module, students learn basic automation concepts such as sequence, control flow, loops, etc. Decision points are explained with examples, and an automation project is discussed using the flowcharts.

in sequences the process steps flow
in a clear succession decision trees are
rarely used
activities in sequences are easier to
read and maintain
thus they are highly recommended for
simple and linear workflows
in flowcharts the individual activities
are
a bit more difficult to read and edit
but the flows between them
are much more clear use flowcharts when

in order to accommodate complex
scenarios workarounds
and decision mechanisms

data manipulation is the process
through which the data is altered using
various operations
including modifying structuring
formatting or sorting
having raw data is not enough and it
needs to be manipulated as per the user

in order to facilitate its usage and

converting extracting splitting

data before it can be used
and it also customizes data according to
users needs
data of all kinds can be stored in
variables

scalar type includes characters
booleans or numbers collection types
include arrays lists and strings
tables are two-dimensional structures

indexed by rows and columns
for example a bulk data such as

data manipulation provides to

it in other documents
or correlated with information from
other sources
common operations for data manipulation

retrieving data specifying conditions to
extract
filter or summarize data
adding data means add new data in a
data structure the user can specify
conditions to limit
the addition only to filtered entries
deleting data removes entries from a
data structure
and modifying data refers to any type of
operation
that affects the existing entries in a
data structure
data conversion is the process of
converting one type of data to another
type
there are several methods to perform
data conversion
first assigning a data value to the
desired
data type second
convert any data type to string using
the
dot to string method
and third using generic value variable
the generic value variable can store any
kind of data
including text numbers dates and arrays
for illustrative examples please refer
to the handouts

string is the data type corresponding to
text
that contains any sequence of text
almost every automation scenario
involves the use of strings
as they are used any time a text needs
to be captured
processed sent between applications or
displayed
some of the operations that can be
performed on strings
are concat concat
concatenates the string representations
of two specified
objects
contains checks whether a specified
substring
occurs within a string and it will
return a boolean value
of true or false
format converts an entire expression
into a string and it can reduce the
complexity
and increases readability
index off returns the zero-based index
of the first occurrence of a character
in a string
zero based index means the first item
is represented with number zero
you can look at the examples listed on
the slide
to better understand the functions of
each method
join concatenates the elements in a
collection
and displays them as a string
replace identifies a sequence of
characters of the string type
in a text and replaces it with a given
string
split splits a string into substrings
based on a certain criteria set by the
user
this criterion could be a space comma
or a full stop
substring extracts a substring from a
string
using the starting index and the length
it is used to isolate a separate
substring
from the original string
you can also look at the examples listed
on the slide
to better understand the functionalities
of each method
and you will also know more about the
functions of each method
by doing the practice in the assignment

modules.name: RPA Fundamentals: UiPath II
modules.description: This module (the second in the UiPath series) continues the explanation of more basic automation tools of UiPath, including user interface, inputs, containers, recording and selectors. The data manipulation of tables for automation purposes is discussed and covered with examples, including table and collection manipulations. The user interface of the UiPath is further explored and useful features of the software are described.

transcript.text:
a data table
is an in-memory representation of a
single database table
which has a collection of rows and
columns
data table stores data as a simple
spreadsheet
with rows and columns so that each piece
of data can be identified
based on their unique column and row
coordinates
in data tables columns are identified
through capital letters or their names
and
rows are identified through numbers
there are multiple ways to create data
tables and
here are some examples build data table
builds a reliable structure for a data
table
it allows the customization of the
number of columns
and the type of data for the each column
read range gets the content of a
worksheet
or a selection from network sheet and
stores it in a data table variable
read csv is very similar to read range
but it works with csv files as
input data scraping enables the user to
extract
structured data from browser application
or
document to a data table
here are some of the manipulations that
we can do to data tables
add data column can add a column to an
existing data table
the input data can be of data column
type
or the column can be added empty by
specifying the data type
and configuring the options
add data row adds a new row to an
existing data table
the input data can be of data row type
or can be entered as
an array row by matching each object
with the data type of
each column merge data table
appends a specified data table to a
current data table
this operation is simpler than the join
data type
activity as it has four predefined
actions to perform
over the missing schema
lookup data table allows searching for
a provided value in a specified data
table
and returns the row index at which it
was found
or can be configured to return the value
from a cell
with given coordinates
filter data table allows filtering a
data table
through a filter wizard using various
conditions
this activity can be configured to
create a new data table
for the output of the activity or to
keep the existing one and filter out the
entries that do not match the filtering
conditions
join data tables combines rows from two
tables
by using values common to each other
it is one of the most useful activities
in business scenarios
where working with more than one data
table is very common
generate data table can create a data
table
from unstructured data by letting the
user
indicate the row and column separators
this option is extremely helpful when
data is captured from scanned documents
or web scraping in order to be able to
process the data
the creation of a data table is the
first step
the activity allows the definition of
separators
but also allows the identification of
elements
using their position on the screen
for each row is similar to for each
loop as it iterates through all the rows
in a data table
and performs the same action this can be
very useful
for manipulations that have to be done
individually
for each row and cannot be grouped at
column level
clear data table clears all the data
entries in a data table
it can be very useful with data tables
that are used as intermediary tables
for data moved from one table to another
output data table is used to write a
specified data table into a csv format
this can serve as an intermediate step
in a process
or as a final step when after several
manipulations
a data table contains only several
values
remove data is a group of two activities
used to remove rows or columns from an
existing data table
rows can be identified by their index
number or by inputting the data row
as object and the columns can be
additionally identified by their names
remove duplicate rows can remove
duplicated entries while keeping
only the first occurrence
sort data table can be used to sort a
data table
using a specified column as the criteria
besides indicating which column to use
in this respect
the user can also choose whether the
sorting
will be done in ascending or descending
fashion

a collection
is used to represent a set of similar
data type items
in a single unit which is used for
grouping
and managing the objects it is useful
in cases when the user wants to extract
the data
in the excel spreadsheet and
orchestrator queues
the different types of collections are
arrays
strings lists and dictionaries
arrays and strings have been discussed
previously
and today's lecture will be more about
lists
and dictionaries
uipath studio offers the following
methods for manipulating collections
add to collection adds an item to a
specified collection
for example it can be used to add a new
name
to a list of company names
remove from collection removes the item
from a specified collection
and can output a boolean variable that
confirms the success of the removal
for example it can be used to remove an
invoice number
from a list of invoices to be processed
exists in collection indicates whether a
given item
is present in a given collection by
outputting a boolean
as the result for example it can be used
to check
whether a list of clients contains a
specific
name clear collection
clears a specified collection of all
items for example it can be used to
empty a collection
before starting a new phase of process
that will populate it again
lists are data structures consisting of
objects of the same data type
for example string or integer
each object has a fixed position in the
list
thus it can be accessed by index
while arrays are fixed size structures
for storing multiple objects lists
allow the user to add insert and remove
items lists
are one of the simplest and the most
used collection data types
lists can store large number of elements
including names numbers and time
coordinates
lists provide specific methods of
manipulation
such as adding and removing items
searching for an element looping through
the items and performing certain actions
on each
sorting objects and extracting items and
converting them to
other data types
a dictionary is a collection of words
and their meanings or definitions
dictionaries consist of key value pairs
in which the keys are unique
here key is equivalent to words
and the value is equivalent to the
meanings
or definitions for example
address book in mobile phone where
each name has corresponding data which
is the phone number or email
the data types for both keys and values
have to be chosen
when the variable is instantiated
data types in dictionaries can be any of
the supported variables
the operations that are most often
associated with dictionaries are
adding and deleting key value pairs
retrieving the value associated with the
key
and reassigning new values to existing
keys
there are several methods for working
with the dictionaries
including initializing a dictionary
adding an item to an existing dictionary
removing an item from a dictionary and
retrieving dictionary items

ui elements
refer to all graphical user interface
pieces
that constitute application such as
windows checkboxes text fields
or drop down lists ui automation can be
easily
implemented by using ui interactions
which are the actions performed by a
user to interact with the system at the
user interface level
there are two types of ui interactions
that can appear in
automation input actions and output
actions
input actions are those actions through
which the user sends data to
application to perform the outcome

and

mouse hover and clipboard actions
output actions are those actions through

from

control shift and when keys

performed on the screen and translating
them into sequences
recording is a crucial tool in uipath
studio
because it captures the step-by-step
activity of the user
it automatically generates the sequence
of all the workflows which are needed
for automation
recording helps in identifying the
elements on the screen
reading data from display and so on
recording helps users to save a lot of
time when
automating their business processes
all user interface elements are
highlighted while recording
to ensure that correct buttons fields or
menus are selected
the recorder is capable of working with
different types of applications
and different types of environments
interactions with ui elements yield
informative screenshots in
automation these can be changed hidden
removed or shown in full size by
selecting the respective
action from the options menu
all screenshots are automatically saved
as picture files in the same location as
the project
in a separate folder named screenshot
in uipath studio there are several user
actions that can be recorded
and these include left clicks on buttons
checkboxes drop down lists and text
typing
however there are some user actions that
are still not supported by the recording
interface and these include keyboard
shortcuts
modifier keys and mouse hovers
the following keyboard shortcuts are
available for use
while recording the f2 key
pauses the recording for three seconds
a countdown timer will be displayed in
the bottom left corner of the screen
this can be very useful with menus that
automatically hide
escape key exits the automatic or manual
recording
and if the escape key is pressed again
the recording is saved as a sequence
and you return to the menu the right
click
exits the recording directly
in uipath there are six types of
recorders
and each comes with its own controllers
to perform specific recording actions
and these are basic recording desktop
recording
web recording image recording citrix
recording
and computer vision recording and these
are discussed in detail in the
subsequent slide
basic recording is used when multiple
actions are performed in a single window
it generates a full selector for each
activity with
no container the resulting automation is
slower than the one that uses container
and is suitable for
single activities it is slower
as compared to the desktop recording
desktop recording is used when multiple
actions are performed in many
windows it is suitable for all types of
desktop apps
such as the notepad it is faster than
the basic recording
and generates a container in which
activities are enclosed and desktop
recording generates
partial selectors for each activity
the web recording is designed for
recording
in web apps and browsers it generates
containers and uses
the simulate type key input method by
default
all these recordings enables the user to
automatically record multiple actions
performed on the screen
such as click type into select item and
check
and manually record single actions
here are some differences between the
basic recording and desktop recording
basic recording is used when multiple
actions are performed
in single window it does not use
containers which means other windows of
the same application
may interfere with the workflow and
basic recording generates full selectors
in some cases multiple windows are
identical
suppose a user opens two untitled
notepad windows at the same time
then if you are using the basic
recording uipath will perform
all action inside the first open window
because the titles of both notepad
windows are the same
that is to say untitled this may not be
a good practice
when you are working on files having
identical names
in contrast desktop recording is used
when multiple actions are performed in
many windows
it nests all activities inside the
attach window container
desktop recording uses containers to
ensure that
other windows of the same type don't
interfere with the workflow
the top level window selectors are
inside the attach window
desktop recording generates a partial
selector for
activities inside the attach window
container
image recording is used to record in
virtualized environments
such as virtual machines and citrix
it permits only image text and keyboard
automation and it requires explicit
positioning
image recording enables the user to
click an image or text
simulate keystrokes or hotkeys select
and copy text from a window scrape
ui elements look for elements and find
the image or wait for it to vanish
native citrix recording is the
equivalent of the desktop recording
but for citrix environment this
is used in native citrix automation
projects
to identify ui elements in citrix apps
and automate them
computer vision is a feature that allows
robots
to see the screen and visually identify
all the elements
rather than relying on selectors or
images

the recording wizards of
all recorders consist of three
components
save and exit record and manual
recording
actions save and exit is enabled only
when the recorder is on
it closes the recorder and reverts to
the designer window
displaying the automatically generated
activities
record starts the automatic recording
mode and
captures user actions on completion
multiple input activities are generated
the user will see a blue screen over the
area
where the action is captured and yellow
outlines for
selectors the remaining actions in the
wizard
are manual recording actions these can
be
common or different for different
recorders
and these are discussed in coming slides
startup is available for basic and
desktop recording
it has two types of activities startup
and close app startup is to open
the application it is similar to the
open application activity
of the uipath studio to do this
select start app and then click on the
desired application to open it
close app is used to close the already
open application
it is similar to the closed application
activity
of uipath studio to use this
select close app and then click on the
desired application to close it
open browser is available for web
recording
it has three activities open browser
close tab and close browser
open browser is used to open a browser
the browser can be chosen
or the url can be specified inside the
quotation marks
or the user can store the url inside the
string variable and pass that inside it
close tab closes the browser tab
specified by user
and close browser closes the already
open browser window
click enables the user to record clicks
on the desktop
or running select an option
from a drop down list or combo box and
select a check box or radio button
it is available in basic desktop
web and native citrix recorders the
activity is supported by the automatic
recorder and it doesn't recognize right
clicks
there are three options in the click
activity
click select item and check
click is to click on the option or
ending button
select item is to select an item from a
combo box
or list box and check is to check or
uncheck
options or to toggle
type includes those actions that require
input from the keyboard
it is also available in all recorders
type contains two types of activities
type and send hotkey type generates the
type into activity and send hotkey
generates the send hotkey activity
copy enables the user to copy a selected
text
from an opened application or browser
it is available in basic desktop web and
native citrix recorders
the copy activity contains copy text
screen scripting and script data
copy text generate get text activity
and recognize the text and copy it for
further use
as it is stored in a variable screen
scraping
is used to read the data from the screen
it
is capable of scraping both visible and
non-visible ui elements from the screen
the screen scraping tool can
automatically detect the position of the
text on the screen
and it has three methods full text
native text and ocr
script data in web recorder allows us to
extract any structured data from the web
aiming application documents and
spreadsheet
element enables the user to perform
actions on elements like
looking for elements or waiting for them
to vanish
it is available in all recorders except
computer vision recorder
element contains mouse element keyboard
element
control element find element and window
element
mouse element enables the user to
simulate mouse movements for an
element keyboard element enables the
user to perform keyboard actions for an
element
control element enables the user to
control a particular ui element
find element enables the user to
identify specific
ui elements or pause the automation
window element enables the user to close
windows
text enables the user to select or hover
over text
it is available in all recorders except
computer vision recorder
text contains elements of mouse
scrape copy text set text
and select and copy mouse generates
click text activities prompting the user
for the ui element to click
scrape generates get text or extract
structured data activities
using screen scraping and data scraping
copy text generates a get text activity
to copy the value of a ui element and
store it in a new variable
set text generates a set text activity
to input
in a ui element select and copy generate
a type into and copy selected text
activity
prompting the user for the ui element to
copy
image enables the user to wait for a
specified
image to disappear to find a specific
image in an app or website right click
or hover over an image and others
it brings together several events to
recognize images
and treat them as triggers image
contains
three actions mouse find image
and weight image vanish
mouse subcategory contains different
methods of using the mouse
in relation with images click double
click
right click and hover by using these
properties
these activities can be customized to
use modifier keys
find image is used to find the image on
the screen and return
a boolean value in the output
wait image vanish waits for the image to
disappear from the screen
another activity starts when the image
is inactive or
invisible from the screen this activity
also has a 30 seconds
timeout
these are some actions that are
available only in the image recorder
and these are click image or click text
select and copy and screen scraping
and these are actions that are available
only in the computer vision recorder
and these are refresh change application
click relative get text and hover

selectors
are a fundamental part of uipath because
they are used to recognize
the ui elements on the user screen
in uipath studio to start or to execute
a specific task in the user interface
the user requires buttons windows
drop down lists and advanced features
which eventually make up the body of
selectors
in uipath studio the selector activities
are available
inside the ui automation activity
in general selectors can be considered
as
the address of a ui element
when a specific task or activity is
automated
the user needs to communicate with
various ui
elements on the screen the selector
is a specific identifier to find a ui
element
among multiple applications on the
screen
selector editor enables the user to
access the automatically generated
selectors
and edit their attributes to access the
selector editor
we can follow these steps first
go to the workflow designer panel
then click on any activity whose
selector is to be edited
and then go to the right hand side of
the properties panel
expand the target option and then you
will see the selector option
to see the selectors you can further
click the three dot button
another way to view selector is to
directly click on the hamburger button
on the activity box and then click
edit selector once we're in the selector
editor panel
we will see four attributes of the
selector editor and they are validate
indicate element repair and highlight
for validate there are four potential
status
the circle means the selector is being
validated
the check mark means the selector is
valid
the cross mark means the selector is
invalid
and the question mark means the selector
has been modified and
it is being revalidated let's watch this
video
to see how to create selectors with the
help of
the basic recorder hello
and welcome to the step-by-step demo of
how to create selectors using the basic
recording feature of uipath studio
the first step is to open a notepad
window
now in uipath studio click on recording
from the design tab
and select basic from the list
from the basic recording window click
type
select the notepad editor enter the text
automation increases efficiency in the
small pop-up window and press
enter on your keyboard
click on type from the drop down menu
and select send hotkey
select the notepad editor from the small
pop-up window
select control and enter a as the key
click ok
now select click from the drop down and
select click
select format from notepad's menu items
in the use anchor popup window select
the check box
and click no
now select click from the basic
recording window
press f2 to pause ui path for 3 seconds
navigate to the format drop-down of the
notepad menu item
and wait for the countdown to end now
click font after the countdown ends
from the type drop down of the basic
recording window select type
and select the text area of the font
section
enter the font name century
check the empty field box and press
enter on your keyboard
repeat this process for the font style
section

check empty field and press enter on

now repeat this process for the size
section
enter the size value as 12 check empty
field
and press enter on your keyboard
select click from the basic recording
window and select the ok button within
the font window
click save and exit from the basic
recording window
you can see that the recording session
has generated a sequence of activities
in the designer panel
in the properties panel of the type into
activity click on the selector property
and from the selector editor window
uncheck the title
now the process will look for any
notepad window
on the screen independent of its title
name
we will learn about the selector editor
in future chapters
now run the process the recorded process
has been successfully executed
this brings us to the end of this
demonstration
this brings us to the end of this
demonstration thank you for watching
goodbye

in uipath studio
selectors are of three types full
selectors
partial selectors and dynamic selectors
full and partial selectors are discussed
here
when automation is created through basic
recorder
four selectors are generated four
selectors start with a window or
a html identifier and they contain all
the information of the ui element
they are suitable when the robot has to
switch through multiple windows
and the use of containers would add
unnecessary complexity
when automation is created through
desktop recorder
partial selectors are generated they do
not contain the top level window
information which is highlighted in grey
on the slide they are more suitable when
the robot has to perform
multiple actions in the same window or
application
in these cases the activities will be
placed
inside a container such as attach window
or
attach browser
partial selectors work only inside a
container
that specifies the top level window
where the elements belong
the advantages of using containers with
partial selectors are
it virtually groups activities that work
on the same application
it is slightly faster because it's not
seeking for the top window every time
it makes it easier to manage top level
selectors
in case manual updates are necessary
and most importantly it helps when
working on
two instances of the same application
a selector helps to capture the
attributes of a particular ui element
on the screen if the same ui element is
present
on another window then a dynamic
selector is used
to easily identify its attributes on
this window
using a variable or an argument a
dynamic selector uses
a variable or argument as a property for
the attribute of the target tag
this allows the selector to easily
identify a target element
based on the value of the variable or
argument
this is best suited for situations in
which the targeted element
constantly changes its value as a
dynamic selector does not identify the
target element
based on the exact stream which might
change
depending on interactions inside the
automation project
the variable or argument can be changed
to interact with a different element
without changing the selector itself
in terms of format when using a dynamic
selector
we can simply copy and paste a line of
the selector
to the editable area and replace the
content
after the equation mark by
the variable that we create and we can
enclose
the variable in the double curly
brackets
wildcards are symbols that enable the
user to replace
zero or multiple characters in a string
when dealing with dynamically changing
attributes in a selector
there are generally two types of wild
cards
the asterisk replaces zero or more
characters
and the question mark replaces a single
character

ui explorer
is an advanced tool that enables the
user to create a custom selector
for specific ui element it can be
accessed from the designer tab
it is available only if the ui
automation activities package
is installed as a dependency to the
project
the interface of ui explorer consists
of four panels and several fields the
four panels are
visual tree panel selector editor panel
selector attribute panel and property
explorer panel the visual tree panel
displays the
tree of the user interface hierarchy and
enables the user to navigate through it
by clicking the arrows in front of each
node
by default the first time ui explorer is
opened
this panel displays all opened
applications
in alphabetical order
selector editor panel displays the
selector for the specified ui object
and enables the user to customize it
selector attribute panel displays all
the available attributes of a selected
node
and the property explorer panel displays
all the attributes that a specified ui
object can have
the fields in ui explorer are validate
indicate element indicate anchor repair
highlight and ui frameworks
validate button shows the status of the
selector
by checking the validity of the selected
definition
and the visibility of the target element
on the screen
indicate element indicates a new ui
element
to replace the previous one indicate
anchor
enables the user to choose an anchor
relative to the target ui element
repair enables the user to re-indicate
the same target ui element
and repair the selector this button
is available only when the selector is
invalid
highlight brings the target element in
the foreground
the highlight stays on until it's
switched off
this button is enabled only if the
selector is
valid ui frameworks
change the technology used to determine
ui elements and their selectors
the user can switch between three ui
frameworks
from the ui explorer designated button
the default framework is the proprietary
method
which usually works correctly with all
types of user interfaces
active accessibility represents an
earlier solution from
microsoft that makes apps accessible
it is recommended when using legacy
software
if the default framework does not work
as expected
ui automation is the improved
accessibility model from microsoft
which is recommended when using newer
applications
in case the default framework does not
work as expected
the selectors generated may be very
different from one framework
to another

anchors are used when a reliable
selector is not available
if a selector keeps changing then the
user defines an anchor
or label that can locate selector every
time
it changes anchor base activity in
uipath
searches for a ui element by using other
ui elements
as anchors so the activity locates
anchor and its relative ui element on a
screen
properties of anchors can be categorized
into three parameters
common input and miscellaneous
under common category there are two
properties
continuum error is used to specify
whether automation should continue
executing the rest of the activities if
the current activity failed
true or false is used to input the
decision for this property
if true or false is not specified for
this property
then automation will use false as the
default value
display name displays the name of the
activity
under input category there is one option
called anchor position this is one of
the key properties of anchor
and we will discuss this in detail in
the next slide
under miscellaneous category there is
one property of
private if this property is selected
then variables and arguments will not be
logged at
the verbals level verbals level logs the
values of variables and arguments
it also makes log entries every time a
process
starts or ends
anchor position is the property under
input category of anchor base
and it is one of the key properties of
anchors
it refers to the edge or position where
the ui element has been anchored
there are six options that can be used
by anchor position property
when auto is chosen the ui element
closest to the container is searched
when there are ui elements in all
directions and at
equal distance then the anchor base
activity will search ui element
in the order of right left and bottom
when left is selected ui element on the
left of the container is
searched similar interpretations
apply to top right bottom and
on top it is good to use anchor position
as
auto and the default direction for the
anchor position
is first right left and then bottom
these directions are generally preferred
for an anchor position
and then the closest one is selected
anchor base is a container that searches
for a ui element
by using other ui elements as anchors
and it is used when a reliable selector
is not
available and we can watch this
demonstrated video of using anchors
for creating selectors
hello and welcome to the step-by-step
demo of how to use anchors for making
the selectors more reliable
for demonstration purposes the
rpacchallenge.com website will be used
in this workflow
in uipath studio drag and drop an open
browser activity
into the designer panel
enter the website url
www.rpachallenge.com
run the workflow
notice the location of the company name
field in the form
run the workflow notice the location of
the company name field in the form
its location changes every time this web
page is refreshed
now go back to the workflow which was
built
insert a type into activity within the
do container of the open browser
activity
click the indicate element inside
browser link and indicate the input
field of the company name label
on the website insert the text uipath
in the input field of the type into
activity
run the workflow notice that the
location of the company name field has
changed this time
also the execution is taking longer than
usual
because the process is facing difficulty
finding the company name
due to the change in its location on the
screen
wait for a few seconds and now you can
see that the selector not found error
appears on the screen to solve this
problem
we must use an anchor base activity it
will help the automation find the
selector
even when it changes location
delete the type into activity
and insert an anchor base activity in
the do container
now insert a find element activity in
the anchor area
of the anchor base activity
click the indicate element inside
browser link and indicate the label
company name as the anchor
next insert a type into activity
in the action activity area
click the indicate element inside
browser link and indicate the input
field
for the company name
run the workflow to test the code
you can see that the position of the
company name field has changed again
but the process succeeds in inserting
the required text in the field
this is the benefit of using anchors
this brings us to the end of this
selector demo
this brings us to the end of this
demonstration thank you for watching
goodbye

modules.name: RPA Fundamentals: UiPath III
modules.description: This module (the third and last in the UiPath series) covers more advanced automation features of the UiPath software, including screen scraping, data scraping and etc. It further illustrates automation of PDF files, Excel workbook and email in detailed steps with examples. The PDF extraction practice is presented as a useful skill for students to master.

transcript.text:
screen scraping is the process
of extracting data from a specified ui
element
or document such as a pdf file
screen scraping methods are the core of
all the activities that enable
extracting data
from a specified ui element or document
in some cases data is sent directly from
the user
or from one activity to another by using
variables or
arguments but in other cases other
methods are needed
to take data out from various screens or
documents
this is where screen scraping methods
are used
they enable data extraction from
different ui elements
that the automation workflows interact
with
the screen scripting wizard enables the
user to point at
a ui element and extract text from it
the steps are first start the screen
scripting wizard
from the design ribbon in the uipath
studio
the screen goes into computer vision
mode
highlighting the ui elements that it
identifies with blue
once the user selects the ui element the
uipath studio will automatically choose
a screen scraping method
after it finishes extracting the text
the wizard
shows the outcome and the user can
select one of the three methods
to customize it using the properties
and then the user can click the refresh
button
to refresh the outcomes according to the
new settings
to extract text from a ui element
the user can use one of the three screen
scraping methods
that uipath supports and these are
the full text method the native method
and the ocr method the full text method
is
the default output method it is the
fastest method with a hundred percent
accuracy
and can work in the background
the full text method can extract hidden
text
for example fetching options available
from a drop down list
however full text method does not
support
citrix which is a virtual environment
and it does not capture text position
and formatting
the native method is compatible with
applications
that use the graphics design interface
the speed of the native method is slower
than the full text
and native method does not work in the
background
however this method can extract the text
position
and formatting with 100 accuracy
and just like the full text method it
does not support
citrix which is the virtual environment
the ocr or the optical character
recognition method
is the only method in uipath which
works with citrix this technology relies
on recognizing each character
just like how we recognize faces in
photographs
ocr cannot work in the background
and it cannot extract hidden text
and the speed of ocr is the lowest
its accuracy varies from one text to
another
and changing settings can improve the
results
just like the native method it can also
capture the position
of the text
the full text output method is the
default output method in uipath
it is fast accurate and can work in the
background
it can capture all the text from the
terminal screen
including the hidden text however
it doesn't work on citrix and other
different virtual environments
it does not retain formatting and text
position as well
the native output method can be used
only with applications
that are built to render text with
graphics device interface
it is useful when the coordinates of
each word
are needed and when the font and color
of the text are required to be retained
accurately
however it has its limitations as well
it only extracts the visible text
and it cannot work in the background and
it cannot support
citrix this method offers the following
options
no formatting and get words info
when the font and color are not
necessary to be captured
the node formatting box can be checked
and the wizard extracts only the text
just like the full text method
for get words info this option can
extract the position of each word and
can support
several separators by default
it can process all known characters as
separators
like comma or space but when specific
separators are specified it ignores
the others additionally the custom
separators field is
displayed enabling the user to determine
the characters
used as separators if the field is empty
all known text separators are used
although both full text and native
methods
have excellent results in terms of
accuracy and
speed there are specific cases in which
both of these methods are unusable
for example if we need to extract
information
from a virtual environment or read text
from images the ocr output method should
be used
it is based on the ocr technology used
in recognition of scanned documents
it attempts to recognize each letter or
given an image in the target document
it is slow when compared to the other
methods and it has
lower accuracy and the accuracy varies
from one text to another
it cannot extract hidden text and cannot
work in the background
the ocr method has two default engines
the use of these engines depends on the
type of
information being extracted and the two
engines are google tesseract
and microsoft moddy in general
it's better to switch between the
methods to see which
of the engines brings the better results
for
each particular situation if the user
has downloaded or
installed the packages of other ocr
engines
such as a b a b b y y
they will also be available while
choosing the ocr engine
google tesseract ocr gets better results
for character recognition on smaller
size areas
and supports color inversion
it offers multiple customization options
through filters that can be used to
select only
specific categories of characters
the method offers the following options
language the default language is english
characters this enables the user to
select
which types of characters to be
extracted
and the following options are available
any character numbers letters
uppercase lowercase and so on
invert when this checkbox is selected
the colors of the ui element are
inverted before scraping
this is useful when the background is
darker than the text color
scale the scaling factor of the selected
ui element or
image the higher the number is the more
enlarged is the image this can provide
a better ocr read and it is recommended
with
small images the get words info
gets the on-screen position of each
script word
microsoft ocr is used to work with
microsoft fonts and on larger size areas
it supports multiple languages this
method offers the following options
language enables the user to change the
language of the script text
by default english is selected
the scaling factor of the selected ui
element or image
can be configured in the scale option
and the higher the number is the more
enlarged
is the image this can provide a better
ocr read
and it is recommended with small images
gets word info gets the on-screen
position of each script word
now let's watch this demonstrated video
on how to scrape text from a uipath blog
post
using the screenscraper wizard and store
that information
in a notepad file hello
and welcome to the step-by-step demo of
how to use the screen scraping tools
in uipath studio for demonstration
purposes
we will build a workflow that will
scrape text from a uipath blog post
and store it in a notepad file
the first step is to open a blog post on
the uipath website
now go to uipath studio and click the
screen scraping button from the design
ribbon
indicate the text you want to copy from
the uipath blog page
in the screenscraper wizard you can see
that we can choose from three different
scraping methods from the drop down menu
these are native full text and ocr
choose each of them one by one to see
their results in the preview area
click the scraping method drop down and
select
native now
click the refresh button and see the
preview you can see in the preview area
that the native method
successfully scrapes the data now try
the other two methods as well
again click the scraping method drop
down
and select ocr and click the refresh
button
we can see that the output of ocr is in
paragraph style as you saw on the
website
now click the scraping method drop down
and select full text
and click the refresh button to see the
preview
you can see that the text is scraped in

pdf

pdf extraction is the process of

are pdf files originally generated from

they are built of code that allows them
to be viewed and read exactly as they

scanned pdfs made up of scanned images

the results can be saved as a text file
and displayed in a message box
other string operations can be used to

extract information out of the generated
text
the activity extracts or converts only
the text part of the document
and any images in the document are
ignored
read pdf with ocr reads all characters
from a specified pdf file
and stores it in a string variable by
using
ocr technology it uses
ocr which stands for optical character
recognition to scan the images inside
the pdf document
and output all the text as a variable
it requires an ocr engine for the
scanning procedure
important property of these activities
is range
it specifies the range of pages that the
user wants to read
the user can specify a single page for
example
7 a range of pages for example
7 to 12 or a complex
range for example 2 to 5
comma 7 comma 5 to
end or or the default value is
or only string variables and strings are
supported
some other activities related to pdfs in
uipath
are get pdf page count
this provides the total number of pages
in a pdf file
export pdf page as image this creates an
image from a page in a specified pdf
file
extract images from pdf this
extracts images from a specified pdf
file
and saves them in a folder extract pdf
page range this extracts text
from a specified range of pages from a
pdf document
join pdf files joins multiple pdf files
stored in our array of string into a
single pdf file
manage pdf password manages the password
of a specified pdf file
if current password details are unknown

the integration with excel
is enabled by using an excel application
scope container
all the other activities used to work
with the specified excel file
are placed inside the container the
purpose of the container is to open the
excel workbook
and provide a scope for excel activities
when the execution ends the specified
workbook
and excel application are closed
it can be configured to write the output
of the activities
in the container in a different file
some important properties of excel
application scope are
workbook path auto save create
new file read only and visible
workbook path specifies the full path to
the excel workbook
autosave saves the file when the
workflow exits the scope
create new file creates a new file if
the one entered does not exist
read only prevents editing or writing to
the file in scope
if visible is checked it opens and
reads the file using microsoft excel
all the activities performed are visible
and multiple operations can be performed
at the same time
here are activities specific to excel
automation
these activities can read from and write
to csv files using data table variables
although found under the excel
application
integration they work even if they are
not placed
inside an excel application scope
container
the activities under this category are
append to csv read csv
and write csv upon to csv
adds the information from a data table
to a csv file
creating it if it does not exist
the activity does not overwrite existing
data
read csv reads all entries from a csv
file and store them in a data table
write csv overrides a csv with
information from a data table
range these activities can read data
insert and delete rows and columns
and even copy and paste entire ranges
they are similar to the corresponding
activities under data table
but they work directly in the excel file
the activities under this category are
delete column
insert column insert or delete columns
insert or delete rows select wrench
get selected range delete range
auto fill range copy and paste range
lookup range and remove duplicate range
delete column removes a column from
excel file based on the name
insert column inserts a blank column in
an excel file
at a certain position insert and delete
columns
either adds blank columns or removes
existing
columns based on the specified change
type
insert or delete rows either adds blank
rows or removes existing rows
based on the specified change type
select range selects a specified range
in an excel file
in general it is paired with another
activity that performs
a certain manipulation over the selected
data
get select to range outputs a given
range as a string
delete range removes a specified range
from an excel file
autofill range applies a given formula
over a given range
in excel file copy paste
range copies and pastes an entire range
from a source sheet to a destination
sheet
lookup range searches for a value in
all the cells in a given range
remove duplicate range deletes or
duplicate rows from a given range
table these activities create filter
and sort tables directly in excel files
the activities under this category are
filter table
sort table and create table
filter table applies a filter on all the
values from a column
in a table inside an excel file
once the file is saved only the rows
that meet the filter will be displayed
this activity does not remove the rows
that do not meet the criteria
but only hides them sort table
sorts a table in an excel file based on
the values in a given column
create table creates a table in a range
specified in the properties panel
file these activities work directly with
the excel files
either by saving or closing them
the activities under this category are
closed workbook
and save workbook close workbook
closes at opened excel workbook and save
workbook
saves changes to the workbook specified
in the workbook path property
of the excel application scope it can
only be used in the excel application
scope activity
cell color these activities are able to
capture and modify the background color
of cells in excel files
the activities under this category are
get cell color
and set range color get cell color reads
the background color or
a given cell in excel file and stores it
as a color variable
output set range color changes the
background color
of all the cells in a given range
the input is a color variable
sheet these activities can perform
various actions over the sheets in
excel file the activities under this
category are
get workbook sheet get workbook sheets
and copy sheet get workbook sheet
reads the name of a sheet by its index
get workbook sheets extracts the sheet
names
and stores them ordered by index
copy sheet copies a sheet in an excel
file
and pastes either in the same excel file
or in a different one specified
pivot table these activities facilitate
working with pivot tables in excel files
the activities under this category are
refresh pivot table and create pivot
table
refresh pivot table refreshes a pivot
table in
excel file this is useful when pivot
table source data changes
as the refresh is not automatic
create pivot table creates a pivot table
using
a specified sheet and given parameters
macro these activities can execute
macros that were already defined
in the excel file or can invoke macros
from other files
these activities work with xslm files
the activities under this category are
execute macro and involve vba
execute macro executes the macro within
a workbook
the workbook file needs to be a macro
enabled workbook
invoke vba invokes a macro from

modules.name: Advanced Topics in Audit Automation: Automation and Data Analytics
modules.description: Big data has become very common in daily life. This module presents the definition and examples of big data and then introduces various data analytics types and tools that implement them. Additionally, the module introduces basic Python programming and descriptive analysis with Python. For Robotic Process Automation, the module introduces the UiPath automation system and combines it with Python.
modules.matched_lecture_name: Advanced Topics in Audit Automation: Automation and Data Analytics

modules.name: Automation Case Study: Billing
modules.description: This module demonstrates the application of Robotic Process Automation (RPA) in accounting via a hands-on billing case using UiPath. It walks students through the requisite steps to map out the major tasks of the process, including flowchart creation, culminating in building a bot for invoice generation for all customers (Part 1) and unbilled customers (Part 2). The configuration of the bot implements the logic of RPA as presented in the flowchart.

modules.name: Fair Value Measure of Crypto Currency
modules.description: This module reviews US regulations and accounting standards which are essential for fair value measurement. Furthermore, it analyzes the possible classification of crypto currency measurements and proposes the current fair value measurement consensus. As an illustration, a quantitative model is applied to evaluate the fair value of crypto currency in a test field whose results are also examined in the module.
modules.matched_lecture_name: Fair Value Measure of Crypto Currency

modules.name: Information Risk Management
modules.description: This module addresses Information Risk Management, including the steps of vulnerability identification and control risk identification in cybersecurity. Moreover, it describes the types of controls using factor analysis for information risk. Students also learn how to use the two-factor quantitative model to assess information risk.

modules.name: Crypto Currency Markets and Ecosystem
modules.description: The crypto currency market has developed quickly in recent years. This module introduces the common crypto currency, exchanges, and other relevant financial concepts in this emerging market. “Coinbase” is a typical example of how investor wallets interact with businesses and banks. The module further describes the centralized and decentralized transaction structures in the market and explains how they form an ecosystem.

modules.name: Basics of Clustering for Audit
modules.description: This module covers basic clustering algorithms (k-means, hierarchical, DBSCAN) and how they run. This technology is widely used in identifying outliers as well as profiling of records (customers, suppliers, users, etc.) Cluster types are explained and their use in the field is discussed. Additionally, limitations of clustering algorithms are explained and methods to assess cluster validity are demonstrated.

modules.name: Basics of Regression Analyses for Audit
modules.description: This module provides basic knowledge about regression algorithms and their potential use for forecasting purposes. The accuracy of forecasting methods are explained, and their evaluations are further discussed. The module implements regression in the audit domain, and illustrates how its results can be used as audit evidence.
modules.matched_lecture_name: Basics of Regression Analyses for Audit

modules.name: Advanced Artificial Intelligence in Accounting and Audit
modules.description: The module introduces three advanced Artificial Intelligence techniques, namely, Smart Analytics, IoT Robotic Process Automation, and NLP speech recognition. Students learn how these techniques are used using machine learning technologies. They also get familiar with use cases in business contexts.
modules.matched_lecture_name: Advanced Artificial Intelligence in Accounting and Audit

lectures.lecture_name: ML Smart Analytics and Levels of Intelligence

modules.name: Basics of Cloud Computing
modules.description: The module provides an understanding of basic cloud computing knowledge, such as computing techniques, types of service delivery models, and deployment models. The module also discusses the characteristics and benefits of cloud computing.

modules.name: Business Adoption Models of Cloud Computing
modules.description: This module expands on the understanding of cloud deployments models. The definitions, purposes, and information about the current market use of deployment models are provided. The module also discusses the benefits and drawbacks of each deployment model.

modules.name: Process Mining with Celonis
modules.description: This module provides a practical demonstration of applying Celonis (a process mining software) to a real-life order-to-cash case. The module demonstrates step-by-step how to start with Celonis, the critical functions of Celonis workspace, dashboard, and visualization. It also shows how to analyze processes using Celonis.

modules.name: Visual Analytics for Audit
modules.description: Visual Analytics is the science of analytical reasoning facilitated by interactive visual interfaces. This module introduces the concepts of incorporating visual analytics in auditing and proposes a four levels Visual Audit model. The practical applications of Visual Audit for auditing professionals, accountants, standard setters, and software designers are further discussed.

modules.name: Intro to ESG and ESG Assurance
modules.description: The field of Environment, Social, and Governance (ESG) emphasizes sustainable business practices and corporations’ responsibility to be effective stewards of natural resources, human resources, and the public trust. ESG has attracted increased attention from regulatory bodies worldwide for its ethical and financial implications, and presents a growing market for accounting firms as the demand for ESG assurance increases. This module offers an introductory overview of ESG reporting from an accounting perspective. Students who complete this module should be conversant in the fundamental concepts underlying ESG reporting, the primary factors motivating the adoption of ESG reporting into corporate practice, some of the ways that ESG-related statistics are being reported, and challenges inherent to assuring ESG-based claims. Research projects from the Continuous Auditing and Reporting (CAR) lab exploring advanced ESG assurance tools are highlighted.
modules.matched_lecture_name: Intro to ESG and ESG Assurance

modules.name: Lifecycle of Cryptocurrency: Accounting, Auditing and Tax Considerations
modules.description: This module discusses the Accounting, Auditing, and Tax consideration of Cryptocurrency from the enterprise’s perspective. The authors not only show the updated guidance from the regulators, but also talk about the current practice in the industry regarding the accounting treatments, auditing practices, and tax filings.

modules.name: Intro to Audit Analytics
modules.description: Audit analytics demonstrates the increasing involvement of more data sources and analytics techniques in the audit process. This module lays out a variety of changes audit analytics can bring to the audit process, including the change in sample size, timeliness, precision and trustworthiness, the scope of assurance, and relevance. These changes are facilitated by full population testing, continuous audit, big data and exogenous data, and non- financial assurance. The lecturers also elaborate on a research project to exhibit how these tools and technologies are applied in a real-life business setting.

modules.name: Data Preprocessing and Manipulations for Audit (Python Basic with Google Colab)
modules.description: Python is one of the most important tools for processing accounting and audit data. This module introduces the basic syntax of Python in Google Colab. The benefit of Colab is that it doesn’t require installation and allows users to open and continue work on a Python project on any computer. The lecturer also shows how to connect Google Drive to Colab and how to debug using Google and Stack Overflow.
modules.matched_lecture_name: Data Preprocessing and Manipulations for Audit (Python Basic with Google Colab)

modules.name: Data Preprocessing and Manipulations for Audit (Python Pandas with Google Colab)
modules.description: This module demonstrates how to use the powerful Pandas data processing tool in Google Colab. The lecturer shows how to do basic data manipulation in Pandas, such as location, merge, drop and sort. At last, it also provides an example of converting categorical data into numerical data using several methods.

modules.name: Audit in Cloud Service
modules.description: The cloud has been a very popular concept in recent years. At the same time, it’s a very wide and deep subject. This module first reviews the basic cloud concepts, tools, and technologies. Then it focuses on introducing different types of cloud service audits. The lecturer also illustrates continuous audit, assurance, and controls in cloud service.

modules.name: Introduction to Machine Learning
modules.description: Machine learning (ML) is the most popular topic of Artificial Intelligence. The massive power of ML in the business world has been proved by more and more scholars. This module introduces the basic ideas of ML, its differences between statistical models, different types, and some evaluation metrics. The author also introduces several common-seen algorithms in detail.

modules.name: Machine Learning Experiments
modules.description: Machine learning (ML) is the most popular topic of Artificial Intelligence. This is a hands-on module that introduces the basic steps in performing ML tasks, including data preprocessing and understanding, training, validation, and testing. Two datasets and several ML algorithms are used as examples for illustration.

modules.name: Basic Text Mining Techniques
modules.description: The business world involves a lot of textual data, such as annual reports, financial news, and conference calls. This module introduces many basic text-mining techniques to preprocess, prepare and analyze textual data.

modules.name: Introduction to Quantum Technologies

modules.name: Quantum Theory Crash Course
modules.description: This is the second module of the quantum technologies series course. This module teaches the fundamental scientific ideas and terminologies needed to understand Quantum technology, literature, and applications. No Scientific background is necessary as this module explains all the basics.

modules.name: Introduction to the Dark Web in Cybersecurity
modules.description: The dark web is a subset of the deep web. In addition to being invisible to standard web search engines, the dark web is the content on the World Wide Web that cannot be accessed without the use of special software or techniques. This module is designed to give an introductory overview of the dark web. The module explains the different layers of the internet with the deepest part known as the dark web. The module also introduces the different market participants that may use the dark web for malicious purposes such as cyberattacks. Finally, the module illustrates how cyberattack can be facilitated through the dark web. A python assignment is included in the module to bring more cybersecurity awareness on the potential security risks associated with company data.
modules.matched_lecture_name: Introduction to the Dark Web in Cybersecurity

lectures.lecture_name: Introduction to the Dark Web in Cybersecurity

modules.name: Exogenous Data in audit analytics
modules.description: A large amount of data brings uncertainty in the big data era. In addition to internal financial data, many new data can be used in audit analytics. This module is designed to give students an overview of types of exogenous data, technologies to analyze exogenous data, and cases of using exogenous data in audit analytics. The module covers four types of exogenous data: social media data, social network data, search volume index, and sensor/IoT data.

modules.name: Introduction to NFT, Metaverse, and their accounting considerations
modules.description: This lecture delves into the world of non-fungible tokens (NFTs), highlighting their unique nature as digital contracts representing both digital and physical assets on the blockchain. The discussion emphasizes the challenges of accounting and auditing in the NFT realm, given their significant rise in value and acceptance in recent years. The lecture also explores the concept of the metaverse, detailing its origin and its current practical applications. The metaverse, a blend of advanced technologies like Industry
modules.matched_lecture_name: Introduction to NFT, Metaverse, and their accounting considerations

modules.name: Introduction to Deep Learning
modules.description: This lecture delves into the evolving landscape of deep learning. The lecture traces the journey from traditional machine learning techniques, which often required manual feature extraction, to the automated nature of deep learning, wherein neural networks learn data representations autonomously from raw data. This shift has led to groundbreaking achievements in various fields, from gaming to biology. With a backdrop of the rapid development of neural network architectures, the surge in big data availability, and the growth of high-performance computing, deep learning is now driving revolutionary changes in areas such as robotics, where algorithms are trained through iterative processes rather than being explicitly programmed.

modules.name: Applying Large Language Model (e.g., ChatGPT) in accounting and auditing
modules.description: This lecture introduces the evolution and potential of large language models, particularly foundational models like ChatGPT. Highlighting a shift towards models that serve as a base for myriad tasks, the discussion explores the training on diverse data sources, the challenges of unlabeled data, and the rising significance of self-supervised learning. With real-world examples, the lecture illustrates the application of these models in areas like auditing, where they can detect anomalies in tabular data. The ability of these models to be fine-tuned using natural language prompts, especially in the domain of audit sampling and anomaly detection, is underlined, showcasing their versatility beyond just language processing.
modules.matched_lecture_name: Applying Large Language Model (e.g., ChatGPT) in accounting and auditing

modules.name: Deep learning hands-on session: Building Neural Network with Google Colab
modules.description: This lecture delves into the realm of neural networks, exploring their intricacies and demonstrate its applications. Beginning with an introduction to artificial neurons, the presentation shifts focus to the history, advancements, and contemporary tools, such as PyTorch, that make neural network development accessible. Emphasis is laid on understanding foundational concepts, like hyperplanes, the Universal function approximation theorem, and the formation of feed-forward networks. Then, the practical hands-on session showcases training an autoencoder neural network using a dataset of journal entries to detect anomalies. By leveraging Google Drive, GPU acceleration, and the PyTorch platform, the lecture demonstrates the process of training, model convergence, and the application of neural networks in auditing by identifying irregularities in financial transactions.
modules.matched_lecture_name: Deep learning hands-on session: Building Neural Network with Google Colab

modules.name: Special topics in ESG assurance
modules.description: This lecture dives deep into the realm of ESG (Environmental, Social, Governance) assurance and its relevance in the accounting sector. Originating from the United Nations' sustainability development goals, the importance of ESG has intensified, necessitating its integration into traditional accounting practices. The discussion spans from historical perspectives of corporate social responsibility in regions like the UK and Europe to the emerging need for advanced technologies in handling vast data associated with sustainability reporting. Various challenges, including non-standardized regulations and the perception gap in reporting, punctuate the lecture, with a nod to the increasing global interest in sustainability and the intricate role of technology in facilitating these disclosures.

modules.name: Dark Web, Data Broker, and Information Security
modules.description: This lecture delves into the intersection of information security, data analytics, and accounting, emphasizing the human-centric aspect of cyber threats. The lecture highlights the risks posed by employees and insider threats, illustrated by studies and data from various sectors. The instructor also introduces the dark web's operations, the vast underworld of data brokers, and the intricate web of data sharing and collection. These brokers collect, bundle, and sell data, often with little transparency to stakeholders. The latter part of the lecture focuses on methodologies to gather data from various web sources, mapping data sources, and ensuring accurate mapping of company information for research purposes.
modules.matched_lecture_name: Dark Web, Data Broker, and Information Security

modules.name: Cybersecurity in Financial Accounting I: Cybersecurity Concepts
modules.description: This lecture delves into the foundational aspects of cybersecurity, equipping you with a comprehensive understanding of key concepts, terminology, and frameworks. This lecture explores the significance of cybersecurity in the context of financial and accounting environments. By grasping cybersecurity principles and recognizing their relevance, you'll be prepared to navigate the evolving landscape of digital security.
modules.matched_lecture_name: Cybersecurity in Financial Accounting I: Cybersecurity Concepts

modules.name: Cybersecurity in Financial Accounting II: Cybersecurity Risk Environment Management
modules.description: This lecture delves into the realm of cybersecurity risk management and governance. This module focuses on understanding the roles, responsibilities, and strategies for effective cybersecurity risk management. By exploring cybersecurity governance principles, threat and incident management, business continuity, and financial accountants' involvement, you will develop the skills necessary to navigate the intricate landscape of cybersecurity risk environment management.

modules.name: Cybersecurity in Financial Accounting III: Cybersecurity Incident Response
modules.description: This lecture focuses on the critical aspects of cybersecurity incident response. This module equips you with the skills and understanding required to effectively manage and respond to cybersecurity incidents. By examining roles, responsibilities, planning frameworks, and post-breach protocols, you will gain a comprehensive insight into handling cybersecurity incidents and ensuring the organization's resilience in the face of emerging threats.

modules.name: Cybersecurity in Financial Accounting IV: Cybersecurity Assurance and Disclosure
modules.description: This lecture delves into the critical realm of cybersecurity assurance and disclosure. Building on the knowledge gained from previous courses, this lecture focuses on the key regulations, frameworks, and responsibilities associated with cybersecurity risk disclosure and assurance. By exploring the roles of organizations, auditors, and regulators, you will gain a comprehensive understanding of the dynamic landscape of cybersecurity disclosure and assurance.
